shodhMemoryId.definition: Optional identifier assigned by an external Shodh memory system for the same knowledge record.
shodhMemoryId.scopeNote: Use when a T2K knowledge item is synchronized with an external memory store; omit it for purely local records.

confidence.definition: Numeric confidence score expressing how strongly the system or operator believes a claim or linkage is correct.
confidence.scopeNote: Use a normalized score that is comparable within this package's workflows; relation-level confidence should qualify the specific assertion rather than the entity as a whole.

verified.definition: Boolean flag indicating that a knowledge record or claim has been reviewed and explicitly confirmed by a trusted workflow.
verified.scopeNote: Verification is stronger than confidence. A high-confidence record may still be unverified.

signatureText.definition: Canonical text used to match recurring trace patterns, failures, or fixes against previously captured knowledge.
signatureText.scopeNote: Keep this compact and match-oriented: stack trace fragments, normalized error text, or reduced signal strings.

signalKind.definition: Classifier describing what sort of signal a signature captures, such as error text, stack frame, tool output, or symptom phrase.
signalKind.scopeNote: This is a lightweight discriminator for retrieval and matching, not a full taxonomy.

languageName.definition: Programming or natural language associated with the scoped knowledge or observed run.
languageName.scopeNote: Use for languages such as Swift, TypeScript, SQL, or English when that context changes applicability.

toolName.definition: Name of the tool, executable, or subsystem whose behavior produced the trace or to which the knowledge applies.
toolName.scopeNote: Examples include bd, TypeDB, xcodebuild, npm, or a specific internal service.

packageName.definition: Package or module name that narrows where a knowledge item, scope, or run is relevant.
packageName.scopeNote: Use when applicability is tied to a particular library, ontology package, or code module.

packageVersion.definition: Version string for the package identified by packageName.
packageVersion.scopeNote: Use when behavior changes across releases and the knowledge is version-sensitive.

filePath.definition: Repository-relative or workspace-relative path that localizes where a knowledge item applies.
filePath.scopeNote: Prefer stable, human-meaningful paths over transient build paths.

symbolName.definition: Function, type, command, target, or other named symbol that narrows the applicability of a scope.
symbolName.scopeNote: Use for the most stable identifier available at the code or tool boundary.

payloadJson.definition: Optional serialized raw payload preserved alongside modeled evidence when important source detail would otherwise be lost.
payloadJson.scopeNote: Use sparingly for opaque or evolving external data; the modeled relations and attributes should still carry the primary semantics.

KnowledgeItem.definition: Abstract reusable knowledge distilled from traces, observations, and prior work rather than a single raw event.
KnowledgeItem.scopeNote: Knowledge items are the central claims in T2K. They carry human-readable description, optional external identity, and links to evidence, signatures, scope, and lifecycle relations.

FailurePattern.definition: A recurring failure mode or symptom pattern that can be recognized across multiple traces or incidents.
FailurePattern.scopeNote: Use for known breakages, characteristic error clusters, or stable problem signatures.

FixPattern.definition: A reusable corrective pattern that resolves or mitigates one or more failure patterns.
FixPattern.scopeNote: A fix pattern describes what to change or apply, not just that an issue was resolved once.

Procedure.definition: An ordered operational recipe for diagnosis, remediation, or verification.
Procedure.scopeNote: Use when the knowledge is fundamentally stepwise and may have prerequisites.

Heuristic.definition: A practical rule of thumb that guides interpretation, prioritization, or next action under uncertainty.
Heuristic.scopeNote: Heuristics are intentionally approximate and should often be supported by evidence or contradicted by exceptions.

Signature.definition: Indexable matching artifact used to connect incoming traces to relevant knowledge items.
Signature.scopeNote: A signature is not the knowledge itself. It is the retrieval hook that helps recognize when the knowledge applies.

Scope.definition: Atomic applicability bundle describing the environment or boundary within which a knowledge item is relevant.
Scope.scopeNote: Scopes should stay factored and composable rather than becoming large free-form context blobs.

EvidenceItem.definition: Observed evidence extracted from traces, runs, logs, conversations, or other raw sources and used to support or derive knowledge.
EvidenceItem.scopeNote: Evidence items preserve enough source detail to justify downstream claims without requiring the entire original trace structure.

ExternalEvidence.definition: Reference to evidence that remains primarily owned by an external system but is linked into T2K for provenance.
ExternalEvidence.scopeNote: Use when the canonical artifact lives in another system such as GitHub, beads, CI, or an observability tool.

Run.definition: Observed execution or tool invocation context in which evidence was produced.
Run.scopeNote: A run anchors evidence to a concrete occurrence, especially when the same knowledge can be extracted from repeated executions.

Tag.definition: Existing gist tag concept reused in T2K to label knowledge items with lightweight memory-oriented categories.
Tag.scopeNote: Use Tag.uniqueText as the tag value; T2K does not define a parallel tag entity.

resolves.definition: Links a failure pattern to a fix pattern that addresses it.
resolves.scopeNote: This is the core problem-to-remedy relation in the ontology. Confidence belongs on the relation because a fix may only probabilistically resolve a failure.

signatureLink.definition: Associates a knowledge item with a signature that can be used to retrieve it from future traces.
signatureLink.scopeNote: Many signatures may point to the same knowledge item, and confidence may vary across those mappings.

prerequisiteOf.definition: Orders knowledge items when one must already be understood or performed before another can be applied effectively.
prerequisiteOf.scopeNote: Most often used for procedures, but it can also express conceptual dependency between heuristics or fixes.

supersedes.definition: States that a newer knowledge item replaces an older one as the preferred current guidance.
supersedes.scopeNote: Use for lifecycle transitions such as updated procedures, corrected heuristics, or modernized fix patterns.

contradicts.definition: Marks two knowledge items as being in tension or direct conflict.
contradicts.scopeNote: Use when guidance cannot both be true in the same context, not merely when one is lower confidence.

supportedBy.definition: Connects a knowledge claim to the evidence that supports it.
supportedBy.scopeNote: Support is provenance, not derivation. Use extractedFrom when the knowledge is distilled from a run/evidence combination.

observedIn.definition: Locates an evidence item within the run that produced or exposed it.
observedIn.scopeNote: This keeps individual evidence items grounded in a specific execution context.

extractedFrom.definition: Captures that a knowledge item was distilled from evidence observed in a specific run.
extractedFrom.scopeNote: This is the raw-trace to distilled-knowledge bridge that gives T2K its core provenance story.

appliesTo.definition: Scopes a knowledge item to the environment, package, tool, file, or symbol context where it is relevant.
appliesTo.scopeNote: Use confidence here when applicability is probabilistic or inferred rather than absolute.

hasMemoryTag.definition: Attaches reusable tags to knowledge items for lightweight grouping, recall, and navigation.
hasMemoryTag.scopeNote: This relation is intentionally simple and complements, rather than replaces, explicit scope and evidence modeling.

evidenceRef.definition: Connects a locally modeled evidence item to its external evidence reference.
evidenceRef.scopeNote: Use when T2K needs provenance back to an external system without copying the full external model into the ontology.
